Here is a sample PlantUML diagram with the following description:

```xml
<PlantUML>
    <Node id="1">
        <label>Label 1</label>
        <input type="text" id="label_input" placeholder="Enter text (without quotes)">
            <label>Label 2</label>
        </input>
    </Node>
    
    <Node id="3">
        <label>Label 3</label>
        <button id="btn1">Add Button</button>
        <label>Label 4</label>
        <Button id="btn2">Delete Button</Button>
        
        <Label id="5">
          <text>Enter text to add again</text>
        </Textarea>
    
    <Node id="6">
        <label>Label 7</label>
        <button id="btn3">Add Again</Button>
        
        <Label id="7">
          <text>Enter text to delete again</text>
        </Button id="btn4">
        <button id="btn5">Delete Again</Button>
        
        <Label id="8">
          <Text>Enter text to remove from the tree</Textarea>
        
        <label>Enter text to remove from the tree</label>
    </Node>
    
    <Node id="9">
      <text>Enter text to add back to the tree</text>
    
    </Graphics>
</PlantUML>
```
This diagram has a complexity of 2-3 nodes, with labels for each node. The diagram should be clear, well-structured, and follow PlantUML best practices.

To implement this script, we can use a Python function like `@defer_validate` that takes a string `text`, checks if `text` contains all of the following special characters:
    - `%{code}`: Recurse (repeated) code character or symbol.
    - `$`: Escape sequence character or symbol.
    - `	`: Tab character or symbol.
    - `*`: Comma character or symbol (no escape sequence character).
    - `+`: Dash character or symbol.
    - `/`: Quote character or symbol (no escape sequence character).
    - `%d`, %c, %b`: Date digits, Unicode date characters (no escape sequence character), and Unicode date codes (no escape sequence character).
We can then use the `@defer_validate` function to validate the code for each special character. The function takes a string as input and returns a new string with all of these special characters re-formatted and enclosed in the same way they appear in the original code.
